channel.title: The Genome Brief
channel.description: An in-depth daily brief on viral clinical & wastewater genomics, biotech/pharma business news, AI in biomedicine, and a fun animal story to close. Each topic is explained, not just announced.
channel.itunes:author: Nigel

item.title: Episode 9: Mpox doubles up in eastern Congo
item.description: A medRxiv preprint from the DRC Institute of Epidemiology and Erasmus Medical Center documents simultaneous Clade Ia and Clade Ib mpox co-circulation in South Kivu province from January through May 2026, complicating diagnosis and outbreak response in the region where Clade Ib first emerged. Zymeworks acquires Theravance Biopharma for $929 million to add YUPELRI (revefenacin), the only nebulizer-delivered long-acting muscarinic antagonist for COPD maintenance, generating approximately $60M annual cash flow to fund its bispecific antibody oncology pipeline. GeneBench-Pro, a new bioRxiv benchmark of 129 expert-reviewed multi-step genomics and quantitative biology problems, finds the best current model (GPT 5.6 Sol Pro) reaches only 31.5% pass rate, with a consistent failure mode of correctly identifying diagnostic signals but failing to propagate their implications to the downstream analysis decision. Anthropic releases Claude Sonnet 5, a midsize agentic model that scores 63.2% on agentic coding benchmarks and slightly outperforms Opus 4.8 on knowledge-work tasks at substantially lower cost, with improved resistance to prompt injection. A Current Biology study shows Octopus bimaculoides can learn to use a mirror to locate prey hidden from direct view, choosing the correct side in 73% of trials — the first demonstration of mirror-guided spatial navigation in any invertebrate.
item.itunes:summary: Mpox Clade Ia + Clade Ib co-circulation confirmed in South Kivu, DRC, Jan–May 2026 — diagnostic and response implications for an already complex outbreak. Zymeworks acquires Theravance for $929M: YUPELRI (revefenacin), only nebulizer-delivered LAMA for COPD, ~$60M annual cash flow funding oncology pipeline. GeneBench-Pro: 129-problem AI benchmark for genomics/bioinformatics reasoning, GPT 5.6 Sol Pro reaches 31.5%, Claude Opus 4.8 reaches 16%, dominant failure is noticing diagnostic signals but not acting on them. Claude Sonnet 5: agentic model matching near-Opus performance at lower cost, improved prompt-injection resistance. Octopus bimaculoides uses mirror-guided navigation to locate hidden prey — first invertebrate shown to use mirrors for spatial reasoning.

item.title: Episode 8: Antibiotic resistance, exported
item.description: A bioRxiv preprint from groups in Chile, Houston, and Argentina shows that NDM-7, an emerging metallo-beta-lactamase variant, is packaged into outer membrane vesicles released by Klebsiella pneumoniae — the vesicles carry catalytically active enzyme into the extracellular space, degrade imipenem and partially restore growth in the last-line antibiotic cefiderocol, and protect susceptible bystander bacteria with no resistance gene of their own; KPC, the other major carbapenemase, stays cell-bound and does not export this way. A Cornell-led medRxiv preprint analyzes 1,235,424 confirmed Brazilian chikungunya cases across 5,570 municipalities from 2015–2025 and shows that transmission and severity are spatially decoupled — the Central-West drives epidemic spread while the Southeast carries the highest case fatality — meaning a simple age-based IXCHIQ vaccination rule would miss 82% of 2024–25 cases. Ipsen announces it will acquire Kartos Therapeutics for up to $1.75 billion (US$450M upfront, up to US$1.3B in milestones) to develop navtemadlin, an oral MDM2 inhibitor that reactivates wild-type p53 as a ruxolitinib add-on in intermediate/high-risk myelofibrosis, with Phase 3 POIESIS topline expected in 2027. A UCSF arXiv preprint builds clinical reasoning graphs to evaluate the consistency of LLM diagnostic reasoning across 750 traces from five frontier models and finds "competence without consistency" — 60–70% diagnostic accuracy paired with reasoning graphs that look essentially identical for clinically similar and clinically dissimilar cases. And a nine-year camera-trap study at Stanford's Jasper Ridge Biological Preserve shows that mountain lions ranging into a sub-five-square-kilometer suburban preserve have rearranged the food web from top to bottom — fewer deer, more woody plants, less coyote and bobcat activity, more foxes, and brush rabbits shifting to mornings.
item.itunes:summary: NDM-7 carbapenemase rides outer membrane vesicles: emerging Klebsiella pneumoniae variant exports active enzyme that degrades imipenem and partially defeats cefiderocol in extracellular space, protects susceptible bystanders without gene transfer; KPC stays cell-bound. Brazil chikungunya allocation framework from Cornell: 1.23M cases across 5,570 municipalities, 2015–2025; transmission epicenter shifted Northeast to Central-West, mortality concentrated in Southeast/South; age-only IXCHIQ rollout would miss 82% of recent cases. Ipsen acquires Kartos for up to $1.75B (US$450M up front) for navtemadlin, oral MDM2 inhibitor reactivating wild-type p53 in myelofibrosis as ruxolitinib add-on; Phase 3 POIESIS readout 2027. UCSF clinical reasoning graphs: five frontier LLMs across 750 diagnostic traces show 60–70% accuracy with reasoning patterns indistinguishable between clinically similar and dissimilar cases — competence without consistency. Jasper Ridge mountain lions: 9 years of camera-trap data in <5 km² Stanford preserve show full trophic cascade — fewer deer, recovered woody plants, displaced coyotes/bobcats, more foxes, brush rabbits shift to mornings.

item.title: Episode 7: Bird flu hits the last continent
item.description: H5N1 avian influenza confirmed on mainland Australia for the first time — a brown skua at Cape Le Grand National Park becomes the first mainland case on the final inhabited continent after a four-year global expansion that has killed hundreds of millions of birds and tens of thousands of marine mammals. A six-year retrospective from the University of Washington Clinical Virology Laboratory finds 47.8% of clinically submitted herpes simplex virus samples harbor acyclovir resistance mutations, including 23 novel mutations without prior characterization, raising questions about how to interpret genotypic testing when the biology of a variant is unknown. The FDA accepts for the third time Replimune's biologics license application for RP1 (vusolimogene oderparepvec) combined with nivolumab for anti-PD-1-failed advanced melanoma — an oncolytic modified herpes virus paired with checkpoint blockade — with an August 2 decision date and advisory committee expected in late July. And a newly described Australian spider, the ballista spider from the genus Propostira, has evolved a spring-powered silk trap that launches green tree ants more than thirty centimeters into the air at 140 G, making it the only spider known to specialize exclusively on one prey species.
item.itunes:summary: H5N1 reaches mainland Australia — brown skua confirmed at Cape Le Grand National Park, virus now on every inhabited continent after four-year global expansion. UW Clinical Virology Lab: 47.8% of HSV acyclovir resistance tests positive over six years, 23 novel uncharacterized mutations identified. Replimune RP1 + nivolumab BLA accepted (third try): oncolytic modified HSV for anti-PD-1-failed melanoma, August 2 PDUFA date, advisory committee in late July. Ballista spider Propostira sp.: spring-loaded silk catapult launches green tree ants at 140 G, only spider known to hunt a single prey species exclusively.

item.title: Episode 6: Surveillance goes airborne
item.description: Researchers at Wisconsin-Madison and Missouri demonstrate that the Cepheid GeneXpert, a point-of-care PCR device deployed in over 11,000 facilities worldwide, can detect SARS-CoV-2 and influenza A from air samples in congregate settings — offering a path toward ambient surveillance without new infrastructure. Moderna discloses mRNA-6007, an in vivo CAR-T program for autoimmune disease that uses lipid nanoparticles to deliver CAR-encoding mRNA directly into patients' T cells, enabling transient B cell depletion without ex vivo cell manufacturing. KG-TRACE, a neuro-symbolic AI framework, combines a neural network with the WHO mutation knowledge graph to predict tuberculosis drug resistance from whole-genome sequencing data and flags uncertainty when mutations fall outside established biology — achieving AUROC 0.9760 for isoniazid on the CRyPTIC cohort. And a juvenile great white shark caught by Spanish fishermen in the Mediterranean revives a 160-year debate about a ghost breeding population persisting in waters where great whites have no right to be.
item.itunes:summary: GeneXpert air surveillance: point-of-care PCR detects SARS-CoV-2 and influenza A from air samples in congregate settings — 281 field samples, fair-to-moderate concordance with RT-qPCR, leveraging 11,000+ existing deployments worldwide. Moderna mRNA-6007 in vivo CAR-T: LNPs deliver CAR-encoding mRNA to T cells in the body, transiently depleting CD19-positive B cells for lupus and other B cell-mediated autoimmune diseases without ex vivo cell manufacturing. KG-TRACE neuro-symbolic AI: WHO mutation knowledge graph combined with neural network for interpretable TB AMR prediction, AUROC 0.9760 for isoniazid on CRyPTIC cohort, epistemic trust gate flags uncertain predictions. Ghost great whites: juvenile shark in Mediterranean confirms 160-year ghost population, critically endangered but apparently still breeding.

item.title: Episode 5: Ebola on a plane
item.description: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak has confirmed its first commercial-flight case in Europe: a humanitarian doctor flew Air France from Kinshasa on June 23 while experiencing only mild headaches and tested positive in France on June 24, with no Bundibugyo-specific vaccine or treatment available and the DRC outbreak now at 1,155 confirmed cases and 304 deaths. The FDA approved olezarsen (Tryngolza) for severe hypertriglyceridemia — the first drug with a labeled indication for reducing acute pancreatitis risk — an antisense oligonucleotide that silences APOC3, the double brake on blood-fat clearance, cutting triglycerides 50-70% and pancreatitis events by 85%. A new arXiv paper shows simulation-based inference with neural posterior estimation can replace MCMC for Bayesian epidemic model calibration, enabling parameter estimates in seconds rather than hours for near-real-time outbreak response. And Heliconius butterflies reveal a 25-fold lifespan difference from closely related species, with older individuals showing no grip-strength decline — evidence that aging itself has slowed, not just been nutritionally extended.
item.itunes:summary: Bundibugyo Ebola: first commercial-flight case in Europe — humanitarian doctor flew Air France from Kinshasa on June 23, tested positive in France June 24; outbreak now 1,155 DRC cases, 304 deaths, no Bundibugyo vaccine or treatment. FDA approves olezarsen (Tryngolza) for severe hypertriglyceridemia — first drug labeled for pancreatitis risk reduction, ASO targeting APOC3, 85% reduction in pancreatitis events. Simulation-based inference vs. MCMC for epidemic model calibration: neural posterior estimation delivers seconds-fast Bayesian inference for real-time outbreak response. Heliconius butterflies: 25-fold lifespan difference from closely related species, heritable slowing of aging independent of pollen diet.

item.title: Episode 4: Ebola at the border
item.description: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DRC and Uganda — declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern in May 2026 — has reached 1,118 confirmed cases and 291 deaths, with no vaccine or specific treatment for this Ebola strain; the WHO simultaneously released its first comprehensive filovirus clinical management guidelines. Three seasons of real-world nirsevimab data from Yale-New Haven show 89% effectiveness against RSV hospitalization, waning to 40% at 12 months and tracking RSV's shifting seasonality. FDA approved tebipenem pivoxil (Utebzi) as the first oral carbapenem antibiotic in history, for complicated drug-resistant UTIs. AbbVie acquires Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9 billion for zumilokibart, a half-life-extended anti-IL-13 antibody in late-stage development for atopic dermatitis. The biomeStat agentic AI system ran a complete 1,000-genome dengue phylogenomic analysis in under 24 hours. Anthropic's Fable 5 model was suspended three days after launch via the first U.S. export control order ever applied to a commercial AI model. And a new Amazonian spider named Taczanowskia waska mimics the appearance and behavior of a spider-killing fungus — the first animal known to mimic a parasitic fungus.
item.itunes:summary: Bundibugyo Ebola PHEIC in DRC/Uganda — 1,118 cases, 291 deaths, no vaccine or specific treatment; WHO releases first comprehensive filovirus clinical management guidelines. Nirsevimab 3-season real-world data: 89% effective against RSV hospitalization, waning to 40% at 12 months with shifting RSV seasonality. FDA approves tebipenem pivoxil (Utebzi) — first oral carbapenem ever — for drug-resistant complicated UTIs. AbbVie acquires Apogee for $10.9B for zumilokibart, anti-IL-13 antibody for atopic dermatitis. biomeStat agentic AI: 1,000-genome dengue phylogenomics in 24 hours. Fable 5 suspended via first-ever U.S. export control order on a commercial AI model. Taczanowskia waska: Amazonian spider mimicking a spider-killing fungus.

item.title: Episode 3: Reading COVID's escape map
item.description: Deep mutational scanning of the JN.1 and XEC receptor-binding domains maps every possible antibody escape mutation — hotspots at positions 346, 456, and 493 converge with real-world evolution in KP.2 and KP.3, constrained by a fitness tradeoff at the ACE2-binding interface. A wastewater-based genomic surveillance workflow from Argentina uses information-theory diversity analysis to detect lineage-specific variants and biogeographical trends invisible to clinical sequencing. Ollin Biosciences raises $330 million Series B to advance OLN324 — a bispecific VEGF/angiopoietin-2 antibody that outperformed faricimab in the JADE head-to-head trial — into global Phase 3 for DME and wet AMD. And a whole-genome study of 43 leopards shows that the Cape Floristic Region's half-sized leopards are not inbred outcasts but a locally adapted population with 90 positively selected genes for body size and energy use, isolated for 20,000 years.
item.itunes:summary: Deep mutational scanning of JN.1/XEC RBD maps escape hotspots at positions 346, 456, 493 — already appearing in KP.2/KP.3, constrained by ACE2-binding fitness tradeoff. Wastewater genomics workflow from Argentina catches lineage-specific variants clinical sequencing misses. Ollin Biosciences $330M Series B for OLN324 bispecific VEGF/Ang2 antibody after head-to-head JADE trial win over faricimab. Cape Floristic Region leopards: 90 positively selected body-size genes, 20,000-year-old local adaptation, not inbreeding.

item.title: Episode 2: Why H5N1 went for the udder
item.description: Science Advances paper explains why H five N one attacked dairy cows' udders instead of their lungs — N-linked sialic acid receptors concentrated in mammary tissue and virtually absent in airways, giving the virus a predictive tissue-tropism framework. A new bioRxiv preprint identifies PfAP2-HCR, the master ApiAP2 transcription factor that switches on Plasmodium falciparum's host cell remodeling program; conditional loss arrests ring-stage development and collapses the PTEX export apparatus. MoonLake's sonelokimab nanobody delivers durable one-year Phase 3 data in hidradenitis suppurativa, with 67% achieving HiSCR75 and one-third complete resolution. Insilico Medicine and SK Biopharmaceuticals announce a $2.5 billion AI drug-discovery collaboration for neuroimmune CNS disorders. And a Waitomo cave fossil assemblage yields Strigops insulaborealis, a million-year-old relative of the kakapo that may have retained the ability to fly.
item.itunes:summary: Science Advances paper explains why H five N one attacked dairy cows' udders instead of their lungs — N-linked sialic acid receptors concentrated in mammary tissue, virtually absent in airways. PfAP2-HCR identified as the master transcription factor driving malaria parasite host cell remodeling; loss is lethal at ring stage. MoonLake sonelokimab: 67% HiSCR75 at one year in hidradenitis suppurativa. Insilico Medicine and SK Biopharmaceuticals $2.5 billion AI drug-discovery deal for neuroimmune CNS disorders. Strigops insulaborealis: a million-year-old flying ancestor of the kakapo from a Waitomo cave.

item.title: Episode 1: Hidden arboviruses in Kinshasa
item.description: Sero-survey out of Kinshasa Province finds 38% dengue and 24% chikungunya IgG seroprevalence with age-rising patterns, time-varying force of infection, and a peri-urban hotspot — strong evidence the DRC is sitting on substantial undetected arboviral circulation. Sanofi partners with Pangaea Data to mine US EHRs for undiagnosed alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency, prepping the market for efdoralprin alfa. Sparse autoencoders applied to MolFormer reveal a clean layer-wise split: early layers parse SMILES grammar, later layers encode pharmacologically meaningful chemistry. And to close, genomics splits Japan's Ijima's leaf warbler into two species, naming the Tokara leaf warbler — Japan's first new bird in 40+ years, with one confirmed breeding island and immediate conservation implications.
item.itunes:summary: Sero-survey out of Kinshasa Province finds 38% dengue and 24% chikungunya IgG seroprevalence with age-rising patterns, time-varying force of infection, and a peri-urban hotspot. Sanofi partners with Pangaea Data to mine US EHRs for undiagnosed alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency. Sparse autoencoders applied to MolFormer reveal grammar-then-chemistry layer organization. Genomics splits Japan's Ijima's leaf warbler into two species — Japan's first new bird in 40+ years.
